extraction.one_line_summary: IMPEY Daryl surprised from reduced bunch after peloton caught break at 10km, launched from 200m out on final climb finish in Annecy
tactical_patterns: Mitchelton-Scott allowed Impey freedom over Edmondson despite GC favoritism, revealing flexible leadership tactics in stage racing.
Peloton deliberately reeled back break only at 10km mark, suggesting confidence in controlling finale without early commitment.
Final climb shredded pure sprinters but punchy climbers Alaphilippe/Bardet positioned perfectly, indicating this profile suits aggressive rouleurs.

rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: Won from 200m out after assuming he'd work for Edmondson, shows opportunistic decision-making under uncertain team orders
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Second after surviving climb but couldn't match Impey's long sprint, suggests power endurance limitation on gradual finales
rider_signals.MOHORIC Matej: neutral: Finished top-5 showing climbing improvement, signals potential stage-hunting versatility beyond pure sprint stages
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: Fourth place with 10-second bonus despite no sprinting reputation, indicates exceptional positioning discipline on reduced finales
